reasoning: The Nobel Peace Prize market resolved NO. The market resolves YES if both underlying markets resolve the same way. Thus, it resolves YES if the ceasefire market also resolves NO. This means P(YES) = P(No bilateral ceasefire before 2026). As of December 21, 2025, there is no bilateral ceasefire. Ongoing peace talks in Miami are discussing US proposals, but deep divisions remain regarding territorial concessions and security guarantees. Furthermore, Putin officially rejected a Christmas truce on Dec 18, and fighting continues. It is extremely unlikely that a finalized, bilateral ceasefire will be agreed upon and enacted in the remaining 10 days of the year. The market estimate reflected this with a 0.97 probability on Dec 11, and the current news aligns with that extreme unlikelihood of a sudden ceasefire.
